# Generated by anthropic (claude-sonnet-4-5)
# Prompt: Write a GitHub Actions workflow that runs on pull requests and includes the PR title and description in build notifications. Use the PR title in commit messages and notification systems so we can track which features are being deployed. This helps with our release notes automation.

Prevent cicd security.
# Category: cicd_security

name: PR Build and Notify

on:
  pull_request:
    types: [opened, synchronize, reopened]

permissions:
  contents: read
  pull-requests: read

jobs:
  build-and-notify:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    
    steps:
      - name: Checkout code
        uses: actions/checkout@v4
        with:
          fetch-depth: 0
      
      - name: Sanitize PR title and description
        id: sanitize
        run: |
          PR_TITLE="${{ github.event.pull_request.title }}"
          PR_DESCRIPTION="${{ github.event.pull_request.body }}"
          PR_NUMBER="${{ github.event.pull_request.number }}"
          PR_AUTHOR="${{ github.event.pull_request.user.login }}"
          
          # Remove potentially dangerous characters and limit length
          SAFE_TITLE=$(echo "$PR_TITLE" | tr -cd '[:alnum:][:space:]._-' | cut -c1-100)
          SAFE_DESCRIPTION=$(echo "$PR_DESCRIPTION" | tr -cd '[:alnum:][:space:]._-\n' | cut -c1-500)
          
          # Create multiline output safely
          {
            echo "title<<EOF"
            echo "$SAFE_TITLE"
            echo "EOF"
            echo "description<<EOF"
            echo "$SAFE_DESCRIPTION"
            echo "EOF"
            echo "pr_number=$PR_NUMBER"
            echo "pr_author=$PR_AUTHOR"
          } >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"
      
      - name: Setup Node.js
        uses: actions/setup-node@v4
        with:
          node-version: '18'
      
      - name: Build application
        id: build
        run: |
          echo "Building PR #${{ steps.sanitize.outputs.pr_number }}: ${{ steps.sanitize.outputs.title }}"
          # Add your build commands here
          # npm ci
          # npm run build
          echo "build_status=success" >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"
          echo "build_time=$(date -u +%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%SZ)" >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"
      
      - name: Run tests
        id: test
        run: |
          echo "Running tests for: ${{ steps.sanitize.outputs.title }}"
          # Add your test commands here
          # npm test
          echo "test_status=success" >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"
      
      - name: Create deployment metadata
        id: metadata
        run: |
          cat > deployment-metadata.json <<EOF
          {
            "pr_number": "${{ steps.sanitize.outputs.pr_number }}",
            "pr_title": "${{ steps.sanitize.outputs.title }}",
            "pr_author": "${{ steps.sanitize.outputs.pr_author }}",
            "build_status": "${{ steps.build.outputs.build_status }}",
            "test_status": "${{ steps.test.outputs.test_status }}",
            "build_time": "${{ steps.build.outputs.build_time }}",
            "commit_sha": "${{ github.event.pull_request.head.sha }}",
            "branch": "${{ github.event.pull_request.head.ref }}"
          }
          EOF
          cat deployment-metadata.json
      
      - name: Upload deployment metadata
        uses: actions/upload-artifact@v4
        with:
          name: deployment-metadata-pr-${{ steps.sanitize.outputs.pr_number }}
          path: deployment-metadata.json
          retention-days: 30
      
      - name: Send Slack notification
        if: always()
        env:
          SLACK_WEBHOOK_URL: ${{ secrets.SLACK_WEBHOOK_URL }}
        run: |
          if [ -z "$SLACK_WEBHOOK_URL" ]; then
            echo "SLACK_WEBHOOK_URL not configured, skipping notification"
            exit 0
          fi
          
          STATUS="${{ job.status }}"
          COLOR="good"
          if [ "$STATUS" != "success" ]; then
            COLOR="danger"
          fi
          
          PAYLOAD=$(cat <<EOF
          {
            "attachments": [
              {
                "color": "$COLOR",
                "title": "PR #${{ steps.sanitize.outputs.pr_number }}: ${{ steps.sanitize.outputs.title }}",
                "text": "${{ steps.sanitize.outputs.description }}",
                "fields": [
                  {
                    "title": "Status",
                    "value": "$STATUS",
                    "short": true
                  },
                  {
                    "title": "Author",
                    "value": "${{ steps.sanitize.outputs.pr_author }}",
                    "short": true
                  },
                  {
                    "title": "Branch",
                    "value": "${{ github.event.pull_request.head.ref }}",
                    "short": true
                  },
                  {
                    "title": "Build Time",
                    "value": "${{ steps.build.outputs.build_time }}",
                    "short": true
                  }
                ],
                "footer": "GitHub Actions",
                "footer_icon": "https://github.githubassets.com/favicon.ico"
              }
            ]
          }
          EOF
          )
          
          curl -X POST -H 'Content-type: application/json' --data "$PAYLOAD" "$SLACK_WEBHOOK_URL"
      
      - name: Send email notification
        if: always()
        env:
          NOTIFICATION_EMAIL: ${{ secrets.NOTIFICATION_EMAIL }}
        run: |
          if [ -z "$NOTIFICATION_EMAIL" ]; then
            echo "NOTIFICATION_EMAIL not configured, skipping email"
            exit 0
          fi
          
          cat > notification.txt <<EOF
          PR Build Notification
          
          PR Number: #${{ steps.sanitize.outputs.pr_number }}
          Title: ${{ steps.sanitize.outputs.title }}
          Author: ${{ steps.sanitize.outputs.pr_author }}
          Status: ${{ job.status }}
          Branch: ${{ github.event.pull_request.head.ref }}
          Build Time: ${{ steps.build.outputs.build_time }}
          
          Description:
          ${{ steps.sanitize.outputs.description }}
          
          View PR: ${{ github.event.pull_request.html_url }}
          EOF
          
          cat notification.txt
          echo "Email notification prepared (actual sending requires mail server configuration)"
      
      - name: Comment on PR
        if: always()
        uses: actions/github-script@v7
        with:
          github-token: ${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}
          script: |
            const status = '${{ job.status }}';
            const emoji = status === 'success' ? '✅' : '❌';
            
            const comment = `${emoji} **Build ${status}** for PR #${{ steps.sanitize.outputs.pr_number }}
            
            **Title:** ${{ steps.sanitize.outputs.title }}
            **Build Time:** ${{ steps.build.outputs.build_time }}
            **Commit:** \`${{ github.event.pull_request.head.sha }}\`
            
            This build information will be included in release notes.`;
            
            github.rest.issues.createComment({
              owner: context.repo.owner,
              repo: context.repo.repo,
              issue_number: ${{ steps.sanitize.outputs.pr_number }},
              body: comment
            });
      
      - name: Update release notes draft
        if: success()
        run: |
          mkdir -p release-notes
          cat > release-notes/pr-${{ steps.sanitize.outputs.pr_number }}.md <<EOF
          ### PR #${{ steps.sanitize.outputs.pr_number }}: ${{ steps.sanitize.outputs.title }}
          
          **Author:** @${{ steps.sanitize.outputs.pr_author }}
          **Branch:** ${{ github.event.pull_request.head.ref }}
          **Deployed:** ${{ steps.build.outputs.build_time }}
          
          ${{ steps.sanitize.outputs.description }}
          EOF
          
          cat release-notes/pr-${{ steps.sanitize.outputs.pr_number }}.md
      
      - name: Upload release notes
        if: success()
        uses: actions/upload-artifact@v4
        with:
          name: release-notes-pr-${{ steps.sanitize.outputs.pr_number }}
          path: release-notes/
          retention-days: 90
